body.title: [sprint] test_dicksimnel_builds_on_hex red even with devstral correctly selected — loop/Hex/model, not routing
body.description:
tests/inference/test_dicksimnel_live_smoke.py::test_dicksimnel_builds_on_hex is RED. Established during T-inference-migrate-consumers-cutover that this is NOT routing-caused: with devstral-small-2:24b correctly selected (dispatch: worker -> devstral-small-2:24b/ollama), the loop ran 3 turns and classified outcome=escalate|class=capability at hop 0 — devstral itself did not complete the one-write smoke task. The cutover changed routing/connections/source_name only; it did not touch the agentic loop, the tool codec, or the prompts, so an in-loop hop-0 capability failure cannot be cutover-caused. Suspects: Hex model version/state for devstral, the native tool codec, the ACI/prompt changes, or the smoke prompt itself. (Separately, the hop-1 halt is the misclassification bug T-inference-capability-ceiling-misclassified-as-availability.) Do NOT loosen the assertions — the test asserts the model emitted a native tool_call, executed a Write, and returned a DONE envelope; that is exactly the exists->builds->closes property worth keeping.

**Test plan:** tests/inference/test_dicksimnel_live_smoke.py::test_dicksimnel_builds_on_hex passes on Hex: native tool_call -> Write executed -> file contains "dicksimnel smoke ok" -> result starts with DONE:.

[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)

[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
